headline: TCS, Infosys retain top spots as Indian IT dominates global brand value ranking
description: NEW DELHI, Jan 20: India’s information technology behemoths continue to dominate the global landscape, with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) and Infosys maintaining their positions as the world’s second and third most valuable IT services brands, respectively, according to the latest ‘IT Services 25 (2026)’ report by Brand Finance. headline: TCS, Infosys retain top positions in global IT brand value rankings
description: India's information technology behemoths continue to dominate the global landscape, with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) and Infosys maintaining their positions as the world's second and third most valuable IT services brands, respectively, according to the latest IT Services 25 (2026)' report by Brand Finance.

headline: Davos 2026: Capability first, revenue later: K Krithivasan on TCS’ AI strategy
description: K Krithivasan, Managing Director and CEO of TCS underlined that AI-led transformation at Tata Consultancy Services is also not about cutting jobs. The company, he said, is focused on building a future-ready workforce through continuous hiring and large-scale reskilling. While some roles may evolve and a few one-off corrections have taken place, AI is being used to enhance productivity and delivery rather than shrink the workforce.

headline: TCS Records ₹52.44 Crore Block Trade on NSE at ₹3,139.50 Per Share
description: Tata Consultancy Services recorded a block trade worth ₹52.44 crores on NSE, involving approximately 167,028 shares at ₹3,139.50 per share. The transaction reflects institutional investor activity and represents significant trading volume in the IT services major's shares.

headline: December quarter earnings muted so far as labour code impact weighs on results
description: So far, around 10 companies from the Nifty 50 index have reported their quarterly earnings, including Infosys, Tata Consultancy Services, HCL Technologies, Tech Mahindra, Wipro, Reliance Industries, HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank, among others.

headline: TCS Establishes Saudi Arabian HQ for Strategic Growth
description: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has established its regional headquarters in Saudi Arabia, investing 1.65 million Saudi Riyals to comply with local bidding norms. This development aligns with Saudi Arabia's requirement for multinational firms to maintain a licensed RHQ in Riyadh for government project eligibility.

headline: TCS Establishes Regional Headquarters in Saudi Arabia with ₹3.30 Crore Investment
description: Tata Consultancy Services incorporated 'Tata Consultancy Services Regional Headquarters' as a wholly owned subsidiary in Saudi Arabia on January 18, 2026, through TCS Netherlands B.V. The company invested 1.65 million Saudi Riyals in cash for 100% shareholding. This strategic move complies with Saudi Arabia's Regional Headquarters Program requirements, which mandate licensed RHQ formation for multinational companies to participate in government project bidding, supporting KSA Vision 2030 objectives.
